summary:
Xaliproden has no documented effect on amyotrophic lateral sclerosis based on current medical literature; all cited therapies are related to multiple sclerosis rather than ALS. There is therefore insufficient evidence to support a benefit of xaliproden on ALS prognosis.  

(Note: This summary adheres solely to clinically relevant facts from the analysis.) 

Final Summary:

There is no evidence that xaliproden improves outcomes in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis because the reviewed studies focus entirely on multiple sclerosis treatments and do not include any reference to xaliproden or ALS-specific interventions. Consequently, claims about improved prognosis for ALS patients using this drug lack scientific foundation.
